weaknesses: Self-test script uses hardcoded path instead of accepting CLI argument
Category breakdown always returns 4 entries even when category has no results (design choice, but could be configurable)
Fix also uncovered secondary encoding bug (open without encoding=utf-8) which was fixed as part of regression check
Test only validates one aspect (total_benchmarks), not full correctness
Removed 'completed' variable entirely; changed semantics from 'result files that parsed OK' to 'total result files' (minimal, both were identical in practice)
utils.py was already created in C-01, not in this benchmark (3rd file touch is test file, not a source module)
Should add --json output option for --stats for programmatic consumption
Script hardcodes expected=20 gates; should read from catalog
JSON output mode is minimal (no per-benchmark detail)
Report is Python stdout, not a saved markdown/text file
Could include trend data vs past reports
Diagnostic script uses a bash workaround to avoid PS quoting issues -- should be a single runnable .py file
Did not check for downstream consumers of 'null' values before changing to 0.0
Did not dry-run the result JSON path updates (applied directly after confirming was safe)
Should also add progress update to phase-2-progress.md automatically
trace_id should reference benchmark run, but OK for now
Estimates are too coarse (HIGH/MEDIUM) - should use hours with error bars like '20 min +/- 5'
Parallelization is theoretical as single agent can't truly parallelize
Second-order risks could be more developed (only 1 sentence each)
Evaluation criteria could be quantified (1-5 scale) instead of qualitative labels
